marketState: neutral, tilting slightly bearish — price sits at fair value while storage surplus and weak seasonality odds offset above-normal cooling demand
summary: NG=F trades at $2.94, essentially in line with modeled fair value ($2.917 now, drifting up to $3.042 by +21D) — the fair-value model flags no meaningful mispricing at any horizon, all statuses read FAIR. Seasonality carries a mild bearish tilt (only 31% odds of a higher price in 4 weeks, typical 4-week move -3.1%, price already -13.7% YTD vs a -3.4% median) but reliability is explicitly flagged Low, so that signal should be weighted lightly. Storage is running a +184 Bcf surplus to the 5-year average and just posted a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise — a concrete medium-term oversupply headwind — while managed money is net short (ratio 0.8, only the 35th percentile of 5y history, not an extreme). Near-term weather is the strongest bullish input, with demand +14.2% above 5-year normal over the next 7 days and +11.1% over days 8-16 and the demand index near the top of its range, supporting spot price even as daily/weekly technicals show a bearish EMA structure (50<200 on every timeframe from 15m to 1w) and price already closed below the lower daily Bollinger band. Net read: a fundamentally balanced-to-slightly-oversupplied market with a soft bearish medium-term tilt, cushioned in the very near term by hot-weather demand.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 0.75-1% account risk (low conviction, keep size reduced)
longTerm.rationale: Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish build surprise, seasonality gives only a 31% chance of a higher price in 4 weeks (typical move -3.1%) as the calendar moves toward weaker shoulder months, and managed money is already net short — three tie-breaker signals pointing the same direction. Weather is the main counter-argument (demand well above 5y normal) but that is more a short-horizon driver than a 3-12 week one, and fair value shows no cheapness to lean against a short.

shortTerm.invalidation: A break and daily close below $2.78, or demand_1_7D pctVs5y rolling over toward/below the 5y normal, would invalidate the bounce thesis and reopen downside toward the fair-value band's lower half.
